original_text:
Iranians celebrating peace and supporters of Field Marshal Asim Munir

Once reports emerged that an Iran-U.S. peace deal was becoming a reality, some hardline religious groups and Pahlavi supporters in Iran took to the streets to protest the agreement. The protests received little attention globally.

Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced news of peace, which was  endorsed by both the U.S. and Iran. It claims that a formal signing ceremony is expected this Friday in Geneva, hosted by Pakistan.

After the announcement, most Iranians expressed gratitude and celebrated. In Iran right now, when people see Pakistanis, they greet them enthusiastically with “Thank you, Pakistan” and “Thank you, Syed Asim Munir” instead of the usual greeting.

یہ صاحب  مہاجرین نشست پر آزاد کشمیر کے وزیر ٹرانسپورٹ ہیں انکی باتوں سے اندازہ لگائیں کہ موصوف خود کتنے تعلیم یافتہ ہیں 
کشمیری ایک پڑھی لکھی قوم کیسے اپنے بچوں کا مستقبل اس طرح کے ڈنگروں کے حوالے کر سکتی ہے 
گفتگو ملاحظہ فرمائیں

**CATEGORY: 2**

*Aoa, sir*  

🔶Subject : *OSINT Update – AJK Transport Minister Video Criticism (15 Jun 2026)*  

🔹**Javed Butt**, **AJK Transport Minister** elected on **refugee seat** (Rawalpindi), faces social media scrutiny over a viral video clip showing his speech delivery and responses during an interaction.  

🔹Clip highlights grammatical/linguistic aspects of his Urdu/Punjabi delivery; poster **@AtharSb12** (21k followers, bio on poverty/crime) mocks him as uneducated "dangar" (donkey) and questions handing Kashmiri future to such figures, tying into ongoing **refugee seats** debate ahead of **AJK elections**.  

🔹Visuals: Man in office chair speaking, gesturing, with TikTok overlays/subtitles emphasizing phrasing on committees, mistakes, and "jung" (war/fight) context. Low engagement (72 views, 6 likes).  

🔶 *Narrative from Official/Pro-Pak*: Refugee seats in **AJK Assembly** represent displaced Kashmiri families' legitimate voice and sacrifices for the cause, protected under constitutional framework. **Javed Butt** serves as a duly elected representative contributing to governance and transport sector; criticism appears aimed at undermining refugee representation rather than policy substance. State institutions and parties emphasize democratic resolution via elections on **27 July**.  

🔶 *Narrative from Anti-State/Propaganda*: Opposition-linked or discontent accounts amplify clips to portray ministers on reserved seats as incompetent outsiders interfering in Kashmiri affairs, fueling demands to abolish the **12 refugee seats** and stoking regional divisions.  

🔶 *Sentiment/Reach*: Negative towards individual minister but low overall reach; reflects broader partisan tensions on refugee quotas amid election season. **@AtharSb12** pushes critical/anti-incumbent view, lacks overt militant ties but aligns with domestic opposition narratives questioning government selections. Official/ISPR-aligned voices counter by stressing unity on Kashmir cause and constitutional validity.  

Links:  
- https://x.com/AtharSb12/status/2066568765731746046 (viral clip source)  

*Regards*
